You can pickpocket items from characters.Name:
You can pickpocket items from characters while hiding. While next to but hidden from a character, you can use the Context Menu to pickpocket, and then select which items you want to steal. You will have to succeed a Sleight of Hand check to successfully steal the items - more valuable items will make the check more difficult. Failing the check will mean you are noticed, and branded a criminal.

[IE_ToggleSpells]Hide
[IE_ContextMenu] Begin pickpocketing

Rogues can do enormous damage against en.Name:
Rogues can do enormous damage against enemies with their Sneak Attack, whether they loose a deadly arrow from afar or sink a blade into the unsuspecting target. However, a Sneak Attack requires that the Rogue either has Advantage on the attack or an ally within melee distance of an enemy. Rogues can make a Stealth check as a bonus action to try and gain Advantage on an attack roll.

You cannot Sneak Attack if you have Disadvantage on the attack roll.
[IE_ToggleSpells] Sneak Attack
Rogues can do enormous damage against en.DisplayName: You cannot Sneak Attack if you have <LSTag Tooltip="Disadvantage">Disadvantage

Use <LSTag Tooltip=\"Inspiration\">Inspi.DisplayName: to redo a failed <LSTag Tooltip="AbilityCheck">Ability Check

If you pick up too many heavy items, you.Name:
If you pick up too many heavy items, you will become Encumbered.

Lightly Encumbered characters will have a reduced Movement Speed and halved Jump distance.
Heavily Encumbered characters move much more slowly, cannot jump or climb, and have Disadvantage on Ability Checks and Attack Rolls, as well as Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution Saving Throws. Drop items or change your equipment to become unencumbered.

[IE_PanelSelect] Open Game Menu
Drop Items
If you pick up too many heavy items, you.DisplayName:
Encumbered.

Lightly Encumbered characters will have a reduced Movement Speed

Thieves' Tools <LSTag Type=\"Image\" Inf.Name:
Thieves' Tools can be used to pick locks if you pass a successful Ability Check. If you fail the check, the Thieves' Tools will break - but worry not, every lock has a key somewhere.

[IE_ContextMenu] Use the Context Menu on a lock or chest to pick it

Lightly Encumbered characters will have a reduced Movement Speed and halved Jump distance.
Heavily Encumbered characters move much more slowly, cannot jump or climb, and have Disadvantage on Ability Checks and Attack Rolls, as well as Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution Saving Throws. Drop items or change your equipment to become unencumbered.

Making a successful <LSTag Type=\"Skills.Name:
Making a successful Stealth check allows you to hide from watchful eyes. While hiding, you can pickpocket characters, sneak around deadly monsters, and get Advantage on attacking certain enemies - essential for a Rogue's Sneak Attack. Suspicious characters will investigate the area you were last visible if you hide from them.

[IE_ToggleSpells]Hide
